title: Expanding slider
abstract: In an interface for controlling a (lighting) device, a slider has a feedback mode for indicating a selected value and an adjustment mode. In the adjustment mode the slider expands to overlap other control function(s) comprised in the interface, those control function(s) arranged for control of the same device which the slider controls. Those other control function(s) are then not available in the adjustment mode. As a result, screen space needed to provide control options can be reduced while at the same time leaving the user with quick access to both the slider function and the other function(s).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method for acquiring user input for controlling an output of a device, the method comprising: providing, via a first area of a screen, a first user interface element (150, 160, 170) arranged for receiving a first user input for controlling a first aspect of the output of the device, providing, via a second area of the screen, a second user interface element comprising a slider (180, 190), the slider arranged for receiving, in an adjustment mode, a value within a range of values as second user input for controlling a second aspect of the output of the device, and the slider further arranged for, in a feedback mode, providing an indication of the received value; and controlling the output of the device according to the received first user input and the received second user input, wherein in the feedback mode the first user input can be received via the first area of the screen, and wherein in the adjustment mode the slider expands in a direction orthogonal to an axis along which the range of values are positioned on the slider such that the second area overlaps the first area and as a result the second user input can be received but the first user input cannot be received. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein the method further comprises: - switching from the feedback mode to the adjustment mode when an input in or near the second area is detected; and switching from the adjustment mode to the feedback mode when the second user input has been received. 3. The method according to any one of claims 1-2, wherein the device is a connected lighting device arranged to provide general illumination. 4. The method according to claim 3, wherein the range of values is one of: a range of intensities, a range of colors, a range of beam patterns, a range of static or dynamic 

 scenes, a hue, a saturation level and a selection of a number of lighting nodes to be controlled. 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the first user input relates to one of: an intensity, a color, a beam pattern, a dynamic lighting effect, an on/off switch, a configuration setting, a scene selection or a menu selection. 6. The method according to any one of claims 1-5, wherein the slider expands in a single direction only. 7. The method according to any one of claims 1-6, wherein the screen is a touch screen and the extent of the expansion of the slider in the adjustment mode is based on the touch imprint in the second area. 8. The method according to any one of claims 1-7, wherein the slider is either a bar slider or a circular slider. 9. The method according to any one of claims 1-8, wherein in the adjustment mode the slider shows all values within the range of values. 10. The method according to any one of claims 1-9, wherein in the feedback mode the slider provides an indication of the received value, but does not show any other values in the range of values. 11. The method according to any one of claims 1-9, wherein in the feedback mode the slider shows all values within the range of values and provides an indication of the received value. 12. A computer program product arranged for performing the method according to any one of the preceding claims when run on a computer device comprising a touch screen, a processor, a memory and an interface between the processor and the device of which the output is controlled. 13. An online software delivery service arranged for electronically delivering to a computer device the computer program product according to claim 12. 14. A computer device comprising a screen, a processor, a memory and an interface, wherein the memory comprises the computer program product according to claim 12, the processor is arranged to run the computer program product, the interface is arranged for controlling the device and the screen is arranged to provide the first and second user interface elements.
